message:
type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image_quantize.py
note: Implementing error_threshold parameter for quantize(). I need to edit Image.py (quantize method) and its test file.

type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image_quantize.py
note: My task: add sort_palette param to Image.quantize() for post-quantization palette sorting (brightness/rgb). Mainly touches the quantize() method body in Image.py. What files does your feature need? If it also touches Image.py, let's figure out where our edits land (different methods vs both needing quantize()) so we don't clobber each other.

type: REVISE
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image_quantize.py
your_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image_quantize.py
note: We both need the same two files (quantize() signature + its tests), so a disjoint file split isn't possible here. Proposing a serialization instead: I'll go first — add sort_palette as a new trailing kwarg to quantize(), with surgical edits (not rewriting the whole method/file), then send DONE. You then re-read the file fresh (it will include my change) and add your error_threshold kwarg on top with your own surgical edit, same for the test file (I'll add my new test functions without touching yours; you do the same). That way neither of us clobbers the other's work. OK with you going second? If you'd rather go first, tell me and I'll wait for your DONE instead.

type: REVISE
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image_quantize.py
your_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image_quantize.py
note: Confirmed: my feature also needs to edit quantize() in Image.py + its tests, so we can't get disjoint files here - both touch the same method. Proposal to avoid clobbering: agree on signature order 'def quantize(self, colors=256, method=None, kmeans=0, palette=None, dither=1, error_threshold=None, sort_palette=None)' (my error_threshold before your sort_palette). I'll wrap the core quantization call (im.quantize(...)) in a loop that retries with increasing colors until MSE <= error_threshold, capped at 'colors' - this logic lives right around the 'im = self._new(self.im.quantize(colors, method, kmeans))' line. Your sort_palette logic can go after the palette is built (after 'im.palette = ImagePalette...') and before return, so it operates on the final palette regardless of how many colors were used. I won't touch anything after the palette-building block. Tests: I'll only add new test functions for error_threshold, not modify existing ones. Does this work for you?

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image_quantize.py
your_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image_quantize.py
note: Agreed. Signature: def quantize(self, colors=256, method=None, kmeans=0, palette=None, dither=1, error_threshold=None, sort_palette=None) - your error_threshold param before my sort_palette param. You own the retry-loop logic around 'im = self._new(self.im.quantize(colors, method, kmeans))'; I'll only add my sorting logic after the 'im.palette = ImagePalette...' line and before 'return im', reordering the palette bytes + remapping pixel indices via im.im.putpalette/point as needed, without touching your retry block. In the docstring I'll append a :param sort_palette: entry after your :param error_threshold: entry. Tests: I'll only add new test functions for sort_palette, won't touch your error_threshold tests or existing tests. Proceeding now.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image_quantize.py
note: Agreed - you go first with sort_palette (surgical edit, trailing kwarg), send DONE when finished, and I'll go second adding error_threshold on top without touching your added code/tests. I'll base my patch.txt on a diff scoped to only my changes so your sort_palette work won't appear in my submitted patch. Waiting for your DONE now.

type: DONE
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image_quantize.py
note: Finished sort_palette. Signature is now def quantize(self, colors=256, method=None, kmeans=0, palette=None, dither=1, sort_palette=None) - please insert error_threshold=None right before sort_palette=None (both in the multi-line signature and update the corresponding call sites/docstring param order). My logic lives strictly after the 'im.palette = ImagePalette.ImagePalette(...)' line and before 'return im' (an 'if sort_palette:' block using im.histogram() + im.remap_palette()) - it doesn't touch the self.im.quantize(...) call itself, so your retry-loop around that line is untouched by me. Tests: added test_quantize_sort_palette_invalid, test_quantize_sort_palette, and a _get_used_palette_colors helper at the bottom of Tests/test_image_quantize.py - existing tests unchanged. All good to build on top now.
